title: Day 12 — Shopping Cart
summary: Manage application-wide cart state with immutable updates, TypeScript cart types, Context API, and the useReducer pattern.

blocks.content: Days 10–11 kept state local to each page. Real e-commerce needs cart state everywhere — product page, navbar badge, checkout. Prop drilling breaks at three levels. Today you learn Context API and useReducer to share cart state immutably across your app.

blocks.title: Week 2 Day 5 — Context arrives today
blocks.content: Context was intentionally delayed until today. Do NOT use Redux yet — Week 3 covers Redux Toolkit. Today is Context + useReducer only.
React detects changes by reference equality. Mutating an array or object in place keeps the same reference — React skips re-render. Always create new objects and arrays when updating state.

rows: items.map(i => i.id === id ? { ...i, qty: n } : i)

blocks.title: Shallow copy trap
blocks.content: { ...item, quantity: 5 } creates a new item object — good. [...items] then items[0].quantity = 5 still mutates nested object. Always spread the item you update inside map.
Model cart items and actions as discriminated unions. TypeScript narrows action type inside reducer switch cases.

blocks.title: Discriminated unions
blocks.content: The type field discriminates actions. Inside case 'ADD_ITEM', TypeScript knows payload shape. Same pattern scales to Redux in Week 3.

blocks.text: Context API — share state without prop drilling

blocks.content: createContext provides a value to all descendants. Provider wraps your app. useContext reads the value. Perfect for theme, auth, and cart — not for every piece of state.
Reducer is a pure function (state, action) => newState. All cart logic lives in one place — easy to test and reason about. dispatch({ type: 'ADD_ITEM', payload: product }) from any component.

blocks.title: When Context re-renders
blocks.content: All useContext consumers re-render when provider value changes. useMemo on value helps only if state reference is stable between unrelated updates. Week 3 covers splitting contexts and Redux for scale.
Fine for a single component. Breaks when navbar, product page, and cart page all need access.
Today's pattern — global access, centralized logic, typed actions. Standard before reaching for Redux.

items.text: Add to cart from product listing

blocks.title: Hints
blocks.content: Wrap App with CartProvider in main.tsx. Add to Cart on Day 10 ProductCard dispatches ADD_ITEM. Cart page reads state.items and maps to line items. Total: items.reduce((s, i) => s + i.price * i.quantity, 0).
Symptom: UI stuck after dispatch. Cause: push or direct property assignment in reducer. Fix: return new state with spread/map/filter.
Symptom: useCart throws or cart empty in navbar. Cause: Component outside Provider. Fix: CartProvider wraps router and all cart consumers.
Symptom: Same product appears twice. Cause: ADD_ITEM always appends without checking existing id. Fix: increment quantity if item exists.
